title: solid-js vs Alternatives: Which Frontend Tool Should You Choose?
description: Compare solid-js with popular Frontend alternatives. See features, performance, and community stats.

sourceData.name: solid-js
sourceData.description: A declarative JavaScript library for building user interfaces.

bodyHtml:
solid-js is making waves in the Frontend ecosystem this week, gaining 2700.7k new stars. With a total of 35.7k stars on GitHub and 1.1k forks, this open-source project is rapidly becoming a go-to tool for developers worldwide.

⭐ 35.7kStars
📈 +2700.7k/wkGrowth
🔀 1.1kForks
⚠️ 63Open Issues

Key Features

  • Written in JavaScript/TypeScript for maximum performance
  • Licensed under MIT — free to use and modify
  • Official website: https://solidjs.com
  • 🏷️ solid
  • 🏷️ solidjs
  • 🏷️ ui
  • 🏷️ reactive
  • 🏷️ components

Why solid-js Is Trending

The Frontend landscape is constantly evolving, and solid-js has emerged as one of the fastest-growing projects this week. Several factors contribute to its momentum:

  1. Growing community: With 2700.7k new stars this week alone, developer adoption is accelerating rapidly.
  2. Active maintenance: The project sees regular updates, ensuring compatibility with the latest ecosystem changes.
  3. Practical utility: A declarative JavaScript library for building user interfaces.

If you're working in Frontend, this is definitely a project worth watching — and adopting.

How It Compares to Alternatives

When evaluating solid-js, it's natural to compare it against established alternatives. Here's what sets it apart:

  • Performance: Built with JavaScript/TypeScript, it leverages modern language features for speed.
  • Community size: 35.7k stars indicate strong community trust and validation.
  • License: MIT licensing means no commercial restrictions.

Final Thoughts

solid-js is a solid addition to any developer's toolkit. With its growing community, active development, and practical design, it's well worth your time to evaluate. Star it on GitHub, try it in a side project, and see if it fits your workflow.
